title: Kotlin Annotations
content:
### Annotations in Kotlin

Annotations are metadata attached to code elements (classes, functions, properties). They provide information to the compiler or runtime.

---

### Declaring Annotations

```kotlin
annotation class Fancy

annotation class JsonField(val name: String)

annotation class Deprecated(
    val message: String,
    val replaceWith: ReplaceWith = ReplaceWith("")
)
```

---

### Applying Annotations

```kotlin
@Fancy
class MyClass {
    @JsonField(name = "user_name")
    var username: String = ""
    
    @Deprecated("Use newMethod()", ReplaceWith("newMethod()"))
    fun oldMethod() {}
}
```

---

### Annotation Targets

Use `@Target` to specify where an annotation can be applied.

```kotlin
@Target(
    AnnotationTarget.CLASS,
    AnnotationTarget.FUNCTION,
    AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY,
    AnnotationTarget.VALUE_PARAMETER
)
annotation class MyAnnotation
```

| Target | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `CLASS` | Class, interface, object |
| `FUNCTION` | Function or constructor |
| `PROPERTY` | Property |
| `FIELD` | Backing field |
| `VALUE_PARAMETER` | Function parameter |
| `EXPRESSION` | Expression |
| `FILE` | File (placed before package) |
| `TYPE` | Type usage |

---

### Retention Policy

```kotlin
@Retention(AnnotationRetention.RUNTIME)  // Available at runtime
@Retention(AnnotationRetention.BINARY)   // Stored in class file, not at runtime
@Retention(AnnotationRetention.SOURCE)   // Discarded after compilation
```

---

### @Repeatable

```kotlin
@Repeatable
annotation class Author(val name: String)

@Author("Alice")
@Author("Bob")
class Project
```

---

### Built-in Annotations

| Annotation | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| `@JvmStatic` | Exposes as Java static method |
| `@JvmOverloads` | Generates Java overloads for default params |
| `@JvmField` | Exposes property as Java field |
| `@Volatile` | Makes field volatile |
| `@Synchronized` | Makes method synchronized |
| `@Transient` | Marks field as transient (serialization) |
| `@OptIn` | Opts into experimental API |
| `@Suppress` | Suppresses warnings |

> **Key Point:** Annotations are a form of metadata. Use `@Target` to restrict where they apply, `@Retention` to control runtime availability, and `@Repeatable` for multiple instances on the same element.

quiz.question: Which annotation target applies to functions?
What does @Retention(AnnotationRetention.RUNTIME) do?
options: Makes annotation available at compile time
Makes annotation available at runtime via reflection
